item.title: Radiotherapeutics & Isotopes in Cancer
item.description:
• The field of radiopharmaceuticals is experiencing a significant moment, with isotopes transforming cancer treatment paradigms. These targeted radiotherapeutics deliver radiation directly to cancer cells by linking radioactive isotopes to molecules that bind to specific tumor antigens. This approach, exemplified by drugs like Pluvicto (lutetium Lu 177 vipivotide tetraxetan) for prostate cancer, offers a potent mechanism for treating metastatic disease. The article highlights the growing pipeline, technological advances in isotope production, and the increasing industry investment in this modality.
  • Varies by agent; typically involves a tumor-targeting ligand (e.g., PSMA binder) conjugated to a radioactive isotope (e.g., Lutetium-177) that delivers localized radiation.
  • BioSpace
